events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: unsealed weatherhead penetration on a commercial service entrance & metering, work in-progress. NEC Art. 230 governs service entrances. An unsealed weatherhead penetration allows moisture/water ingress into the service raceway/equipment, which is a hazard. NEC 230.90? Actually weatherhead sealing relates to 230.54? Let me recall: NEC 230.54 covers service heads (weatherheads). 230.54(A) requires service-drop conductors to be arranged so water won't enter the raceway. 230.54(F) maybe requires sealing. Also 300.5/300.7 for sealing raceways to prevent water accumulation. Unsealed penetration is non-compliant and installed-defective.

Severity: moisture ingress into service equipment can cause short/ground fault, corrosion, equipment failure, shock/fire. High risk.

Decision: fail. Risk: high. Work stage: in-progress. Percent complete: maybe 60? It's in progress. Let's estimate 50.

Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant.

Findings: unsealed weatherhead penetration allowing water/moisture ingress into service raceway and equipment; violates NEC 230.54 (service head arrangement to prevent water entry) and general sealing requirements.

Actions: stop work, seal weatherhead penetration with approved sealing compound/method per manufacturer and NEC; inspect raceway for water; verify drip loop and conductor arrangement; re-inspect.

References: NEC Art. 230 (230.54), NEC 300.7 maybe.

Let's produce JSON.
